task: Building on https://github.com/entireio/cli/pull/692 add a "logout" command corresponding to the "login" command which effectively removes the locally stored credentials and undoes all other local state modifications so the user is in the same state as if they never had run "login" before.
When I run `entire login` I'm only getting `https://entire.io/cli/auth` as the URL to be opened, but not with the auth code parameter - help me understand why and propose a fix
user_feedback_text: When I run `entire login` I'm only getting `https://entire.io/cli/auth` as the URL to be opened, but not with the auth code parameter - help me understand why and propose a fix

task:
Verify this issue exists and fix it:

In `waitForEnter`, when the context is cancelled, `tty.Close()` is called explicitly on line 200 to unblock the reader goroutine, but `defer tty.Close()` on line 188 will also run when the function returns. This double-close of `tty` is a correctness bug — after the first close releases the file descriptor, another goroutine could reuse that FD number, and the deferred close would then silently close an unrelated file.

Affected files: cmd/entire/cli/login.go:199-200, cmd/entire/cli/login.go:187-188
user_feedback_text:
Verify this issue exists and fix it:

In `waitForEnter`, when the context is cancelled, `tty.Close()` is called explicitly on line 200 to unblock the reader goroutine, but `defer tty.Close()` on line 188 will also run when the function returns. This double-close of `tty` is a correctness bug — after the first close releases the file descriptor, another goroutine could reuse that FD number, and the deferred close would then silently close an unrelated file.

Affected files: cmd/entire/cli/login.go:199-200, cmd/entire/cli/login.go:187-188

task: think about security concerns regarding the login flow - if we're presenting a URL to the user which directly includes the auth code - and also opening the complete URL with the auth code in the users browser - what kind of phishing scenarios do you see and what would you recommend as a mitigation?

an alternative to this would be to just always use `verification_uri` i.e. open what we're showing to the user. please implement this approach, dropping the use of `verification_uri` and we'll handle the rest on the service side
